title: ExtendingCDMGsWith
lean_code_with_comments:
/-- The *extended CDMG* of `G` with respect to a subset of nodes
`W ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V`: a new CDMG over the carrier `α ⊕ ↑(W \ G.J)`
obtained by minting a fresh intervention-input node
`I_w := Sum.inr ⟨w, _⟩` for every `w ∈ W \ G.J` and a fresh
directed edge `I_w → w := (Sum.inr ⟨w, _⟩, Sum.inl w)`. The
original input, output, and bidirected-edge sets are carried
verbatim under the canonical `Sum.inl` embedding -- matching the
LN's convention `I_j := j` for `j ∈ G.J ∩ W` (no fresh node, no
fresh edge for existing inputs). See
`lecture-notes/lecture_notes/graphs.tex` definition
`def:cdmg_intervention_nodes` (def 3.13 of the LN).

The four `@[simp]` projection / membership lemmas
`extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_J`,
`extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_V`,
`mem_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_E`,
`mem_extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes_L` below characterise the
four components of the result and are the gateway for every
downstream rewrite. -/
noncomputable def extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes
    (G : CDMG α) (W : Set α) (hW : W ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V) :
    CDMG (α ⊕ ↑(W \ G.J)) where
  J := Sum.inl '' G.J ∪
    Set.range (Sum.inr : ↑(W \ G.J) → α ⊕ ↑(W \ G.J))
  V := Sum.inl '' G.V
  disjoint_JV := by
    rw [Set.disjoint_left]
    rintro x hxJ ⟨v, hv, rfl⟩
    rcases hxJ with ⟨j, hj, hjv⟩ | ⟨w, hwv⟩
    · cases Sum.inl_injective hjv
      exact Set.disjoint_left.mp G.disjoint_JV hj hv
    · exact nomatch hwv
  E := (fun p : α × α => ((Sum.inl p.1 : α ⊕ ↑(W \ G.J)), Sum.inl p.2)) '' G.E
     ∪ Set.range (fun w : ↑(W \ G.J) =>
         ((Sum.inr w : α ⊕ ↑(W \ G.J)), Sum.inl (w : α)))
  E_subset := by
    rintro ⟨a, b⟩ h
    rcases h with ⟨⟨v₁, v₂⟩, hE, hab⟩ | ⟨w, hab⟩
    · -- piece 1: original directed edge (v₁, v₂) ∈ G.E, double-inl-lifted.
      simp only [Prod.mk.injEq] at hab
      obtain ⟨rfl, rfl⟩ := hab
      obtain ⟨hv₁, hv₂⟩ := G.E_subset hE
      refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
      · -- source Sum.inl v₁ ∈ J_ext ∪ V_ext
        rcases hv₁ with hJ | hV
        · exact Or.inl (Or.inl ⟨v₁, hJ, rfl⟩)
        · exact Or.inr ⟨v₁, hV, rfl⟩
      · -- target Sum.inl v₂ ∈ V_ext
        exact ⟨v₂, hv₂, rfl⟩
    · -- piece 2: fresh edge (Sum.inr w, Sum.inl w.val) for w ∈ ↑(W \ G.J).
      simp only [Prod.mk.injEq] at hab
      obtain ⟨rfl, rfl⟩ := hab
      refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
      · -- source Sum.inr w ∈ J_ext (the range-of-inr summand)
        exact Or.inl (Or.inr ⟨w, rfl⟩)
      · -- target Sum.inl w.val ∈ V_ext = Sum.inl '' G.V.
        -- w.property : (w : α) ∈ W \ G.J, so (w : α) ∈ W, (w : α) ∉ G.J;
        -- by hW, (w : α) ∈ G.J ∪ G.V; ruling out G.J leaves G.V.
        have hwW : (w : α) ∈ W := w.property.1
        have hwJ : (w : α) ∉ G.J := w.property.2
        rcases hW hwW with hJ | hV
        · exact absurd hJ hwJ
        · exact ⟨(w : α), hV, rfl⟩
  L := (fun p : α × α => ((Sum.inl p.1 : α ⊕ ↑(W \ G.J)), Sum.inl p.2)) '' G.L
  L_subset := by
    rintro ⟨a, b⟩ ⟨⟨v₁, v₂⟩, hL, hab⟩
    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq] at hab
    obtain ⟨rfl, rfl⟩ := hab
    obtain ⟨hv₁V, hv₂V⟩ := G.L_subset hL
    exact ⟨⟨v₁, hv₁V, rfl⟩, ⟨v₂, hv₂V, rfl⟩⟩
  L_irrefl := by
    rintro a₁ a₂ ⟨⟨v₁, v₂⟩, hL, hab⟩
    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq] at hab
    obtain ⟨rfl, rfl⟩ := hab
    intro heq
    exact G.L_irrefl hL (Sum.inl_injective heq)
  L_symm := by
    rintro a₁ a₂ ⟨⟨v₁, v₂⟩, hL, hab⟩
    refine ⟨(v₂, v₁), G.L_symm hL, ?_⟩
    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq] at hab ⊢
    exact ⟨hab.2, hab.1⟩
  disjoint_EL := by
    rw [Set.disjoint_left]
    rintro p hE ⟨⟨u₁, u₂⟩, huL, rfl⟩
    rcases hE with ⟨⟨v₁, v₂⟩, hvE, hvb⟩ | ⟨w, hvb⟩
    · -- piece 1 of E meets L: both p sides are (Sum.inl _, Sum.inl _),
      -- so Sum.inl-injectivity descends to (v₁, v₂) = (u₁, u₂) ∈ G.E ∩ G.L.
      simp only [Prod.mk.injEq] at hvb
      obtain ⟨h1, h2⟩ := hvb
      cases Sum.inl_injective h1
      cases Sum.inl_injective h2
      exact Set.disjoint_left.mp G.disjoint_EL hvE huL
    · -- piece 2 of E vs L: source Sum.inr w cannot equal Sum.inl u₁
      -- (constructor mismatch).
      simp only [Prod.mk.injEq] at hvb
      exact nomatch hvb.1
lean_code_without_comments:
noncomputable def extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes
    (G : CDMG α) (W : Set α) (hW : W ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V) :
    CDMG (α ⊕ ↑(W \ G.J)) where
  J := Sum.inl '' G.J ∪
    Set.range (Sum.inr : ↑(W \ G.J) → α ⊕ ↑(W \ G.J))
  V := Sum.inl '' G.V
  disjoint_JV := by
    rw [Set.disjoint_left]
    rintro x hxJ ⟨v, hv, rfl⟩
    rcases hxJ with ⟨j, hj, hjv⟩ | ⟨w, hwv⟩
    · cases Sum.inl_injective hjv
      exact Set.disjoint_left.mp G.disjoint_JV hj hv
    · exact nomatch hwv
  E := (fun p : α × α => ((Sum.inl p.1 : α ⊕ ↑(W \ G.J)), Sum.inl p.2)) '' G.E
     ∪ Set.range (fun w : ↑(W \ G.J) =>
         ((Sum.inr w : α ⊕ ↑(W \ G.J)), Sum.inl (w : α)))
  E_subset := by
    rintro ⟨a, b⟩ h
    rcases h with ⟨⟨v₁, v₂⟩, hE, hab⟩ | ⟨w, hab⟩
    ·
      simp only [Prod.mk.injEq] at hab
      obtain ⟨rfl, rfl⟩ := hab
      obtain ⟨hv₁, hv₂⟩ := G.E_subset hE
      refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
      ·
        rcases hv₁ with hJ | hV
        · exact Or.inl (Or.inl ⟨v₁, hJ, rfl⟩)
        · exact Or.inr ⟨v₁, hV, rfl⟩
      ·
        exact ⟨v₂, hv₂, rfl⟩
    ·
      simp only [Prod.mk.injEq] at hab
      obtain ⟨rfl, rfl⟩ := hab
      refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
      ·
        exact Or.inl (Or.inr ⟨w, rfl⟩)
      ·

        have hwW : (w : α) ∈ W := w.property.1
        have hwJ : (w : α) ∉ G.J := w.property.2
        rcases hW hwW with hJ | hV
        · exact absurd hJ hwJ
        · exact ⟨(w : α), hV, rfl⟩
  L := (fun p : α × α => ((Sum.inl p.1 : α ⊕ ↑(W \ G.J)), Sum.inl p.2)) '' G.L
  L_subset := by
    rintro ⟨a, b⟩ ⟨⟨v₁, v₂⟩, hL, hab⟩
    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq] at hab
    obtain ⟨rfl, rfl⟩ := hab
    obtain ⟨hv₁V, hv₂V⟩ := G.L_subset hL
    exact ⟨⟨v₁, hv₁V, rfl⟩, ⟨v₂, hv₂V, rfl⟩⟩
  L_irrefl := by
    rintro a₁ a₂ ⟨⟨v₁, v₂⟩, hL, hab⟩
    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq] at hab
    obtain ⟨rfl, rfl⟩ := hab
    intro heq
    exact G.L_irrefl hL (Sum.inl_injective heq)
  L_symm := by
    rintro a₁ a₂ ⟨⟨v₁, v₂⟩, hL, hab⟩
    refine ⟨(v₂, v₁), G.L_symm hL, ?_⟩
    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq] at hab ⊢
    exact ⟨hab.2, hab.1⟩
  disjoint_EL := by
    rw [Set.disjoint_left]
    rintro p hE ⟨⟨u₁, u₂⟩, huL, rfl⟩
    rcases hE with ⟨⟨v₁, v₂⟩, hvE, hvb⟩ | ⟨w, hvb⟩
    ·

      simp only [Prod.mk.injEq] at hvb
      obtain ⟨h1, h2⟩ := hvb
      cases Sum.inl_injective h1
      cases Sum.inl_injective h2
      exact Set.disjoint_left.mp G.disjoint_EL hvE huL
    ·

      simp only [Prod.mk.injEq] at hvb
      exact nomatch hvb.1
lean_explanation:
The Lean encoding mirrors the LN's four-item construction line by line. `α : Type*` is the ambient vertex carrier (the LN's silent assumption that the graph is over some node type), `G : CDMG α` is the input causal directed mixed graph $G = (J, V, E, L)$, and `W : Set α` is the LN's set of intervention targets $W \subseteq J \cup V$. The hypothesis `hW : W ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V` discharges that subset condition exactly.

The result `extendingCDMGWithInterventionNodes G W hW` lives over a *new* carrier `α ⊕ ↑(W \ G.J)` — the disjoint union of the original vertex type with a subtype `↑(W \ G.J)` (Lean shorthand for `{v : α // v ∈ W \ G.J}`) labelling exactly the fresh intervention inputs. The two `Sum` constructors play the LN's two roles: `Sum.inl v` carries the original vertex $v$ verbatim, and `Sum.inr ⟨w, hw⟩` is the fresh $I_w$ for $w \in W \setminus J$. The LN's convention $I_j := j$ for $j \in J \cap W$ becomes a *type-level* fact: no `Sum.inr ⟨j, _⟩` exists in the carrier for $j \in G.J \cap W$ because the subtype skips them.

A few Mathlib idioms used throughout: `Sum.inl '' G.J` is `Set.image Sum.inl G.J`, the canonical lift of `G.J` into the new carrier; `Set.range (Sum.inr : ↑(W \ G.J) → α ⊕ ↑(W \ G.J))` is the image of the entire subtype under `Sum.inr`; the prefix `↑(W \ G.J)` is the subtype-coercion shorthand for `{v : α // v ∈ W \ G.J}`; and `Disjoint` (used in the structural obligations) is Mathlib's lattice-theoretic disjointness predicate, unfolded via `Set.disjoint_left` to the more familiar pointwise form.

Structurally, the definition bundles four data fields (`J`, `V`, `E`, `L`) and six proof obligations (`disjoint_JV`, `E_subset`, `L_subset`, `L_irrefl`, `L_symm`, `disjoint_EL`) inherited from the `CDMG` structure. The data follows LN items i.) — iv.) literally: `J` is the union of `G.J` (lifted via `Sum.inl`) with the fresh intervention-input range `Set.range Sum.inr`; `V` is `G.V` (lifted); `E` is the union of `G.E` (lifted as a product image under `Sum.inl × Sum.inl`) with the fresh edges `(Sum.inr w, Sum.inl (w : α))` for `w ∈ ↑(W \ G.J)`; `L` is `G.L` (lifted as a product image). The proof obligations close by `Sum.inl`-injectivity, by `nomatch` on `Sum.inl`/`Sum.inr` constructor mismatch, and — for the fresh edge's target-in-`V_ext` obligation — by combining `w ∉ G.J` (from the subtype) with `hW` to force `w ∈ G.V`.
design_choices:
**Carrier `α ⊕ ↑(W \ G.J)` rather than `α ⊕ ↑W`.** The LN names the fresh-node index set as $W \setminus J$, not $W$. Subtyping the `Sum.inr` summand by `↑(W \ G.J)` makes the Lean carrier match the LN's fresh-node set exactly: no `Sum.inr ⟨j, _⟩` exists in the carrier for $j \in G.J \cap W$. This encodes the LN's $I_j := j$ convention *at the type level*, not as a runtime equation on a larger carrier. The alternative `α ⊕ ↑W` would mint a ghost `Sum.inr ⟨j, _⟩` for every $j \in G.J \cap W$ that is never used in any CDMG field; every downstream rewrite would then have to case-split on $w \in G.J$ to identify those ghosts with `Sum.inl j` (or carry them as junk through every membership lemma). A more elaborate alternative — a quotient of `α ⊕ ↑W` collapsing `Sum.inr ⟨j, _⟩ = Sum.inl j` on $G.J \cap W$ — would lose `DecidableEq` and complicate every downstream lemma; rejected.

**`Sum.inl` / `Sum.inr` constructor encoding.** Constructor-disjoint, so the "no collision" structural obligations (`disjoint_JV`, `disjoint_EL`, and the source-vs.-target case-split inside `E_subset`) close by `nomatch` on `Sum.inl = Sum.inr` mismatches rather than by hand-rolled case splits. The original carrier $\alpha$ is losslessly embedded as `Sum.inl '' α` — the gateway for stating the LN-style component characterisations in set-builder notation.

**Precondition `hW : W ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V` is structurally required.** This mirrors the LN hypothesis exactly and is load-bearing for the construction itself: the fresh edge `(Sum.inr ⟨w, hw⟩, Sum.inl w)` has target `Sum.inl w`, which must lie in $V_{ext} =$ `Sum.inl '' G.V` to satisfy the `CDMG`-structure obligation `E_subset`. For $w \in W \setminus G.J$, `hW` gives $w \in G.J \cup G.V$; ruling out $w \in G.J$ (by $w \in W \setminus G.J$) leaves $w \in G.V$. Without `hW`, the construction would not type-check.

**`V` and `L` carried verbatim under the `Sum.inl × Sum.inl` lift.** LN items ii.) and iv.) say $V_{do(I_W)} = V$ and $L_{do(I_W)} = L$. The canonical `Sum.inl`-image lift is the smallest faithful transport from `Set α` and `Set (α × α)` into the new carrier, and no special-casing for $w \in W$ overlap is needed because the extension touches only $J$ (new inputs) and $E$ (new edges), never $V$ or $L$.

**`noncomputable`.** The underlying CDMG sets `G.J`, `G.V`, `G.E`, `G.L` are `Set α`, so the `where`-block's images, unions, and ranges are noncomputable. No caller ever *evaluates* the construction at runtime — every downstream use is `Prop`-valued — so the implicit classical choice has no observable cost.

**Concrete intervention nodes (`Sum.inr ⟨w, _⟩`) rather than opaque symbols.** The Lean shape commits to a concrete identity for $I_w$ rather than treating it as an opaque symbol satisfying an abstract interface. No downstream proof relies on $I_w$'s identity being abstract (they all pattern-match on `Sum.inr`), so this concreteness is a feature: pattern-matching on `Sum.inl` / `Sum.inr` lets every downstream lemma reduce to the two LN cases ("original vertex" vs. "fresh intervention input") mechanically.
